title: test: merge & parameterize redundant test cases
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Consolidate redundant / over-granular tests surfaced by the test-suite audit (every distinct assertion preserved):

- Collapse the 5 per-country Stripe checkout-cohort tests into one parameterized `test.for` (keeps each cohort's metadata wiring; leaves the MO spoof-regression test untouched)
- Table-drive the 6 single-field `buildUsageHeaders` tests into one case (literal wire-keys that `track.ts`/`text-cache.ts` read raw are preserved)
- Merge the two `validateInputModalities` "allows image" tests into one `it.each`
- `customer.test.ts`: collapse the 6-tier passthrough loop to one iteration and delete the redundant zero-balance test
- `pricing-data.test.ts`: reframe around `formatPricePer1M` decimal branching (guards the IEEE-754 rounding bug) instead of restating registry prices
- `docs.test.ts`: replace two volatile prose substrings with a stable section heading

Net −218 lines, 6 files. biome clean; all affected suites pass (Stripe still runs all 5 cohorts).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the test suite
gist.blurb: We swept through the test suite and consolidated redundant checks using parameterized tests. Less code, same coverage, happier developers.
gist.summary: Consolidated redundant test cases across the Stripe, customer, and generation suites using parameterized tests, removing 218 lines of code while preserving all assertions.
gist.impact: Developers benefit from a cleaner, more maintainable test suite without losing any coverage for critical billing and generation logic.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot (Polly) organizing or cleaning up a testing area, sweeping up redundant code blocks or organizing them into neat, parameterized boxes. The Nomnom creature eating the deleted lines of code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping up scattered code blocks into neat, organized boxes. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a pile of discarded test scripts. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
